findings.title: completeJob can overwrite a concurrent state transition
findings.evidence: completeJob validates against a pre-read state at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/ops/job-store.ts:334, but the final UPDATE at line 341 only filters by id, unlike setJobState's state-checked update at lines 244-247. A concurrent cancelled/failed/complete transition can be overwritten by the later completeJob write.
findings.recommendation: Make completeJob use the same optimistic state predicate as setJobState, e.g. UPDATE ... WHERE id = ? AND state = ?, then throw a transition conflict when changes === 0. Also avoid rewriting terminal same-state rows unless explicitly intended as an idempotent no-op.

findings.title: Crash reset bypasses transition rules
findings.evidence: resetRunningJobsForKind accepts options.to as any JobLifecycleState at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/ops/job-store.ts:352 and bulk writes that value to every queued/running row at lines 364-367. This bypasses ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS, allowing invalid persisted transitions such as queued -> complete or running -> queued if the exported API is called with those valid TypeScript values.
findings.recommendation: Narrow the reset target type and runtime validation to the crash-recovery terminal states the store actually supports, or validate options.to against every selected row with isTransitionAllowed before updating.

findings.title: Late cancel can mark a completed post-processing scan as cancelled
findings.evidence: memory-index.ts:1159 is the last scan-side cancellation check before post-processing; lines 1164-1357 can still run stale cleanup, backfills, invalidation, and record success, but handleMemoryIndexScan later chooses the terminal job state from the live cancel flag at memory-index.ts:1432. A cancel requested during any awaited post-processing step can therefore produce state='cancelled' with a completed result/status and completed side effects.
findings.recommendation: Derive the terminal job state from the scan response itself, e.g. only set 'cancelled' when runIndexScan returned the cancelled envelope, and add explicit cancellation checks inside/after awaited post-processing stages if cancellation is intended to stop those stages.

findings.title: Privacy guard allows raw text in allowed fields
findings.evidence: assertCorpusPrivacy only rejects a 16-hex fingerprint in classKey at shadow-replay-corpus.ts:119, then only checks that class object keys are in ALLOWED_CLASS_KEYS at shadow-replay-corpus.ts:122-123. Because classKey and weight are allowed keys, a corpus object with classKey or weight carrying raw query text would pass the fail-closed guard as long as syntheticQuery is a static seed.
findings.recommendation: Make the guard validate allowed-field values, not just keys: require classKey === `class:${intent}:${resultCountClass}`, require weight to be a finite non-negative number, and reject unexpected top-level corpus keys/payloads before returning a corpus.

findings.title: Shipped dist still runs retired replay-pool path
findings.evidence: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/package.json:23 starts node dist/context-server.js, but .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/dist/lib/feedback/shadow-evaluation-runtime.js:284 still calls loadRecentSearchQueries and lines 110-133 still implement the old raw query_text replay pool, while the current TypeScript source builds a synthetic corpus at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/feedback/shadow-evaluation-runtime.ts:389-399.
findings.recommendation: Rebuild and commit the mcp_server dist artifacts so the shipped entrypoint uses buildSyntheticReplayCorpus/buildHoldoutReplayMap, removes loadRecentSearchQueries from dist JS and d.ts, and preserves the null relevance-query path.

findings.title: Privacy guard misses raw text in classKey
findings.evidence: assertCorpusPrivacy only rejects a 16-hex fingerprint in classKey at shadow-replay-corpus.ts:119 and then only checks allowed field names at :122-123. The tests cover a fingerprint classKey at shadow-replay-corpus-privacy.vitest.ts:86-102, but a classKey like `class:fix_bug:SENSITIVE-bananaphone-7f3` with a valid static syntheticQuery would currently pass the guard.
findings.recommendation: Validate classKey exactly equals `class:${intent}:${resultCountClass}` and add a negative SENTINEL-in-classKey test.

findings.title: Cancel test does not prove cancellation prevents indexing
findings.evidence: The cancel test parks before worker execution at handler-memory-index-scan-jobs.vitest.ts:371-375, requests cancel at :389, releases at :392, and only asserts terminal cancelled state plus lease release at :393-394. It never asserts `mockIndexMemoryFile` or mtime updates stayed untouched, so removing the pre-file cancellation guard in memory-index.ts:975-978 would still let this test pass after indexing the file.
findings.recommendation: After releasing the gate and observing cancellation, assert `mockIndexMemoryFile` and `mockBatchUpdateMtimes` were not called for this pre-file cancellation case; optionally add a multi-file partial-cancel case.

findings.title: SQLITE_BUSY test does not exercise UPDATE retry
findings.evidence: The test uses one global `writeCallCount` and two failures at job-store.vitest.ts:69-80. `createMaintenanceJob` at :94 consumes both injected SQLITE_BUSY failures, so `setJobState` at :99 runs after the counter has already exceeded `FAILURES_BEFORE_SUCCESS`; removing retry from UPDATE paths would not fail this test.
findings.recommendation: Split INSERT and UPDATE busy injection or reset/use per-SQL counters before `setJobState` so an UPDATE throws SQLITE_BUSY before succeeding.

findings.title: Scan job tools are registered but missing from layer map
findings.evidence: TOOL_DEFINITIONS registers memory_index_scan_status and memory_index_scan_cancel at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tool-schemas.ts:943-945, but the L7 tools list at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/architecture/layer-definitions.ts:115 includes memory_index_scan and related maintenance tools without the two new scan job tools. The guard at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/layer-definitions.vitest.ts:156-162 expects every registered tool to have a layer definition.
findings.recommendation: Add memory_index_scan_status and memory_index_scan_cancel to the L7 Maintenance tools list so TOOL_LAYER_MAP and getLayerForTool stay aligned with TOOL_DEFINITIONS.

findings.title: Stale tool count guard still expects 37
findings.evidence: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/review-fixes.vitest.ts:117 asserts TOOL_DEFINITIONS.length is 37, while the current canonical parity test asserts 39 at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/spec-memory-cli-parity-and-help.vitest.ts:126 and the committed ownership fixture includes memory_index_scan_status/cancel at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/fixtures/tool-ownership-map.json:145-158.
findings.recommendation: Update this count guard to 39 or derive it from the canonical expected tool list to avoid a stale hard-coded count.

findings.title: Late cancel can mark a completed scan as cancelled
findings.evidence: `runIndexScan` only checks cancellation before entering post-processing at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-index.ts:1159, then can still return a successful result with `status: 'complete'` at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-index.ts:1376. The background runner later chooses the terminal job state solely from `isCancelRequested(jobId)` at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-index.ts:1432, so a cancel accepted during post-processing can persist `state: 'cancelled'` with a complete result.
findings.recommendation: Derive the terminal state from the actual scan response, e.g. only store `cancelled` when the response data status is `cancelled`; otherwise store `complete`/`failed` and leave `cancelRequested` as metadata for late requests.

findings.title: Review finding IDs remain in comments
findings.evidence: Current comments embed shipped-review identifiers: `// Fix F21 — ...` at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:337, `// A7-P2-1: ...` at line 743, and `// Fix F8 — ...` at line 1176.
findings.recommendation: Remove the finding IDs and keep only durable rationale in the comments, or delete the comments if the code is self-explanatory.

findings.title: Feature-catalog labels in comments
findings.evidence: Current source contains tracking-style comments at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-index.ts:76 and :77: `// Feature catalog: ...`.
findings.recommendation: Remove these feature-catalog labels from code comments or move durable ownership metadata outside executable source comments.

findings.title: Feature-catalog label in comment
findings.evidence: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-index-scan-jobs.ts:23 contains `// Feature catalog: Workspace scanning and indexing (memory_index_scan)`.
findings.recommendation: Remove the feature-catalog label from the comment or replace it with durable implementation rationale only if needed.

findings.title: Feature-catalog label in comment
findings.evidence: .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/architecture/layer-definitions.ts:4 contains `// Feature catalog: 7-layer tool architecture metadata`.
findings.recommendation: Remove the feature-catalog label from the code comment; the module header and exported constants already describe the file purpose.

findings.title: Feature-catalog labels in comments
findings.evidence: Current source contains tracking-style comments at .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:60, :61, and :62: `// Feature catalog: ...`.
findings.recommendation: Remove these feature-catalog labels from code comments or keep only durable rationale that explains behavior.
